title: [INFRA] Set up Codespaces dev container
body:
## Objective
Provide a reproducible, zero-setup development environment via GitHub Codespaces so the project can be worked on from any machine without manual Python/dependency configuration.

## Problem
The repo currently has no `.devcontainer` config. Every new environment requires manual setup of Python, pip installs, and `.env` creation — error-prone and inconsistent.

## Design
- Add `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` with Python 3.12, auto-install of dependencies via `pip install -e .[dev]`, and recommended VS Code extensions (Pylance, Ruff, GitLens)
- Add `.devcontainer/post-create.sh` to copy `.env.example` to `.env` on first launch
- Add `.env.example` with placeholder keys so contributors know what's required

## Acceptance Criteria
- Codespace launches and `python main.py` runs without any manual setup
- `.env.example` exists at repo root with `RA_USERNAME` and `RA_API_KEY` placeholders
- Pylance and Ruff extensions are pre-installed in the Codespace

## Implements
RA-INFRA-001

title: [INFRA] Migrate from requirements.txt to pyproject.toml
body:
## Objective
Replace the bare `requirements.txt` with a proper `pyproject.toml` to enable installable packaging, dev dependency separation, and tool configuration in one place.

## Problem
`requirements.txt` has no dev/prod separation, no version pinning strategy, and no project metadata. Tools like Ruff and pytest cannot be configured centrally.

## Design
- Add `pyproject.toml` with `[project]` metadata, `[project.dependencies]` (requests, pandas, openpyxl, python-dotenv), and `[project.optional-dependencies]` for dev (pytest, ruff, mypy)
- Configure Ruff linting rules in `[tool.ruff]`
- Keep `requirements.txt` as a frozen lockfile generated from `pip freeze` for CI reproducibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- `pip install -e .[dev]` installs all dependencies cleanly
- `pyproject.toml` contains Ruff config
- `requirements.txt` is removed or replaced by a pinned lockfile

## Implements
RA-INFRA-002

title: [INFRA] Add README, LICENSE, and CONTRIBUTING
body:
## Objective
Bring the repo up to a standard that clearly communicates the project purpose, setup steps, and contribution workflow — matching the documentation standard of opensvf.

## Problem
The repo has no README, no LICENSE, and no CONTRIBUTING guide. Anyone landing on it (including future-self) has no idea what it does or how to run it.

## Design
- `README.md`: project description, feature list, quickstart (clone → set .env → run), screenshot of sample Excel output
- `LICENSE`: MIT
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: branching strategy (feature branches → PR → main), issue workflow, code style (Ruff)

## Acceptance Criteria
- README renders correctly on GitHub with at least: description, prerequisites, quickstart steps
- LICENSE file is present
- CONTRIBUTING references the issues.json workflow

## Implements
RA-INFRA-003

title: [INFRA] Add CI workflow (lint + test)
body:
## Objective
Add a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs on every push and PR to catch regressions and enforce code style automatically.

## Problem
There is no automated validation. A bad commit can silently break the scanner or API client with no feedback until manual testing.

## Design
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: triggers on push/PR to main
- Steps: checkout → setup Python 3.12 → install deps → run Ruff → run pytest
- Add CI badge to README
- Add a `tests/` directory with at least one smoke test for ROMScanner and one for RAClient (mocked)

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI passes on a clean checkout
- Badge visible in README
- Ruff reports zero violations on current codebase

## Implements
RA-INFRA-004

title: [CORE] Fix console ID bug in main.py
body:
## Objective
Correct the hardcoded console ID passed to `get_console_game_hashes()` which currently causes GBA ROMs to be matched against the wrong console's hash list.

## Problem
The comment in `main.py` says 'GBA Console ID is 4' but the code passes `5`, which is the Game Boy (GB) console. This means every GBA hash lookup silently returns wrong results.

## Design
- Move console definitions to `config.py` as a named dict: `CONSOLES = {4: 'GBA', 5: 'GB', ...}`
- Replace the magic number `5` in `main.py` with `CONSOLES` lookup
- Verify correct IDs against the RA API docs

## Acceptance Criteria
- GBA ROM matching uses console ID 4
- Console IDs are never magic numbers in main.py or anywhere else
- `config.py` CONSOLES dict covers at least: GB (5), GBC (6), GBA (4), SNES (3), NES (7), PS1 (12)

## Implements
RA-CORE-001

title: [CORE] Refactor main.py — extract hash matching to matcher.py
body:
## Objective
Thin out `main.py` to pure orchestration by extracting the hash-map building and ROM matching logic into a dedicated `matcher.py` module.

## Problem
`main.py` currently contains business logic (building `hash_map` from the RA game list, iterating hashes, normalising strings) that does not belong in an entry point. This makes the logic hard to test and reuse.

## Design
- Create `src/ra_manager/matcher.py` with a `HashMatcher` class
- `HashMatcher.build_map(ra_game_list)` → returns `{md5: title}` dict
- `HashMatcher.match(df, hash_map)` → returns df with `ra_title` and `ra_game_id` columns
- `main.py` becomes: init → fetch → match → export, max ~20 lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- `main.py` contains no hash iteration or string normalisation logic
- `HashMatcher` is unit-testable in isolation
- Matching result includes both `ra_title` and `ra_game_id` (needed for progress fetching in M3)

## Implements
RA-CORE-002

title: [CACHE] Implement local hash list cache
body:
## Objective
Add a caching layer that stores the RA console hash lists locally so the API is only called when the cache is stale, eliminating the slow full re-fetch on every run.

## Problem
Every run calls `API_GetGameList.php` which fetches thousands of hashes per console. This is slow (~2–5s per console), burns rate limit quota, and makes the tool unusable when offline.

## Design
- Create `src/ra_manager/cache.py` with `load_cached(key)` and `save_cache(key, value)` functions
- Storage: JSON file at `data/cache.json` keyed by `console_{id}`
- TTL: 24 hours (configurable in `config.py`)
- `RAClient.get_console_game_hashes()` checks cache before making API call
- CLI flag `--refresh-cache` to force a full re-fetch

## Acceptance Criteria
- Second run with warm cache completes hash lookup with zero API calls
- Cache invalidates automatically after TTL
- `--refresh-cache` flag bypasses cache and updates it
- Cache file is listed in `.gitignore`

## Implements
RA-CACHE-001

title: [CACHE] Implement user progress cache
body:
## Objective
Cache per-game user achievement progress locally to avoid re-fetching progress data for games that haven't been played recently.

## Problem
Once M3 adds `get_user_progress()`, fetching progress for every matched ROM on every run will be very slow and will hit RA rate limits for large collections.

## Design
- Extend `cache.py` to support `progress_{game_id}` keys
- TTL: 1 hour for progress data (shorter than hash list since it changes more frequently)
- `RAClient.get_user_progress()` checks progress cache before API call
- Cache entries store: earned count, total count, last_earned timestamp, points

## Acceptance Criteria
- Progress data for unchanged games is served from cache
- Progress cache TTL is independently configurable from hash cache TTL
- Cache size stays bounded (prune entries for game IDs no longer in the local collection)

## Implements
RA-CACHE-002

title: [SCANNER] Improve scanner performance and extension handling
body:
## Objective
Fix correctness issues with how certain ROM file types are hashed and improve scanning speed for large collections.

## Problem
Three issues exist in the current scanner: (1) chunk size of 4096 bytes is unnecessarily slow for large ISOs; (2) `.cue` files are text descriptors — hashing them will never match RA; (3) `.zip` files hash the archive, not the ROM inside, so they will never match RA hashes.

## Design
- Bump chunk size from 4096 to 65536 (64 KB)
- Remove `.cue` from `supported_extensions`; handle `.bin` + `.cue` pairs by hashing only the `.bin`
- For `.zip`: extract in-memory and hash the contained ROM file (using `zipfile` stdlib)
- Add a `skipped` column to the output DataFrame for files that were intentionally skipped with a reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- `.cue` files are not hashed; their paired `.bin` is hashed instead
- `.zip` files are extracted in-memory and the inner file is hashed
- Scanning a 1 GB ISO is measurably faster with the new chunk size
- No third-party dependencies added for zip handling

## Implements
RA-SCAN-001

title: [SCANNER] Add multi-console support
body:
## Objective
Allow the scanner and matcher to operate across multiple consoles in a single run, with each console's ROMs stored in its own subfolder.

## Problem
The current flow is hardwired to a single console ID. Running against a full ROM library with GBA, GB, SNES, PS1, etc. requires multiple manual runs and produces separate CSV files with no unified view.

## Design
- Scanner infers console from folder name using `CONSOLES` map in `config.py`
- `main.py` iterates over all detected consoles in the ROM directory
- Hash matching is done per-console (fetches the correct RA hash list for each)
- Output DataFrame has a `console` column populated from folder name

## Acceptance Criteria
- A ROM library with `roms/gba/`, `roms/gbc/`, `roms/snes/` is processed in one run
- Each ROM row in the output includes the correct console name
- Unknown console folders are logged as warnings but do not crash the run

## Implements
RA-SCAN-002

title: [API] Add get_user_progress() to RAClient
body:
## Objective
Expand `RAClient` with a method to fetch per-game achievement progress for the configured user, enabling the achievement tracking feature.

## Problem
`RAClient` only has `get_console_game_hashes()`. There is no way to retrieve how many achievements a user has earned for a given game, which is a core feature of the project.

## Design
- Add `get_user_progress(game_id: int) -> dict` using `API_GetGameInfoAndUserProgress.php`
- Returns: `{earned: int, total: int, points_earned: int, points_total: int, is_mastered: bool}`
- Add `timeout=10` to all `requests.get()` calls
- Raise `RAClientError` (custom exception) instead of bare `except Exception`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `get_user_progress(game_id)` returns correct earned/total counts for a known game
- All API calls have a 10-second timeout
- `RAClientError` is importable and catchable by callers
- Method is covered by a unit test with a mocked HTTP response

## Implements
RA-API-001

title: [API] Add get_user_summary() to RAClient
body:
## Objective
Fetch the user's overall RA profile stats (total points, rank, games played) to power the summary sheet in the Excel output.

## Problem
There is no way to display high-level user statistics like global rank or total points earned — both of which were listed as desired features.

## Design
- Add `get_user_summary() -> dict` using `API_GetUserSummary.php`
- Returns: `{points: int, softcore_points: int, rank: int, games_played: int, last_activity: str}`
- Cache result with a 1-hour TTL

## Acceptance Criteria
- `get_user_summary()` returns the correct points and rank for the configured user
- Result is cached and does not re-fetch within TTL
- Used in the Summary sheet of the Excel export (M4)

## Implements
RA-API-002

title: [OUTPUT] Implement Excel export with openpyxl
body:
## Objective
Replace the CSV export with a structured, multi-sheet Excel workbook that presents the full ROM library, achievement progress, and stats in a readable format.

## Problem
The current CSV export is flat and single-console. There is no summary view, no per-console breakdown, and no visual distinction between matched/unmatched ROMs or completed/in-progress games.

## Design
- Create `src/ra_manager/exporter.py` with an `ExcelExporter` class
- Sheet 1 — **Summary**: user stats (points, rank), total ROMs, matched %, games mastered, in-progress count
- Sheet 2-N — **Per-console sheets**: one sheet per console with columns: filename, ra_title, matched, achievements_earned, achievements_total, completion_%, is_mastered
- Sheet N+1 — **Want to Play**: manually maintained list, imported from `data/want_to_play.csv` if it exists
- Conditional formatting: green rows for mastered, yellow for in-progress, red for unmatched

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `main.py` produces `data/ra_collection.xlsx`
- Each console has its own sheet named after the console
- Summary sheet shows correct aggregated stats
- Want to Play sheet is present (empty if no source file exists)
- Conditional formatting renders correctly in Excel and LibreOffice Calc

## Implements
RA-OUT-001

title: [OUTPUT] Add want-to-play list management
body:
## Objective
Allow the user to maintain a 'want to play' list that is tracked alongside the ROM library and surfaced in the Excel output.

## Problem
There is no way to flag games of interest that aren't yet in the local collection. This was listed as a desired feature from the start.

## Design
- `data/want_to_play.csv` as the source of truth (manually edited)
- Columns: `ra_game_id`, `title`, `console`, `notes`, `added_date`
- Exporter reads this file and merges with RA game data to populate achievement counts
- Games in want-to-play that are also in the local collection are flagged as 'owned'

## Acceptance Criteria
- `data/want_to_play.csv` is read if present; gracefully skipped if absent
- Want to Play sheet shows achievement total for each game (fetched from RA)
- Games present in both the local collection and the want-to-play list are marked 'owned'
- `want_to_play.csv` template with correct headers is committed to the repo

## Implements
RA-OUT-002

title: [API] Add get_game_hashes() to RAClient
body:
## Objective
Expand `RAClient` to fetch accepted ROM hashes for a specific game to suggest correct dumps.

## Design
- Add `get_game_hashes(game_id: int) -> list[dict]` using `API_GetGameHashes.php`
- Cache result with a 24-hour TTL

## Implements
RA-DEV-026

title: [MATCH] Add fuzzy matching to suggest correct dumps
body:
## Objective
Suggest the closest RA-accepted dump by fuzzy title match for unmatched ROMs.

## Design
- Create `suggest_matches(unmatched_df, ra_game_list)` in `matcher.py`
- Use `difflib.get_close_matches` to map original filename to expected RA Title

## Implements
RA-DEV-013, RA-DEV-014

title: [OUTPUT] Add Unmatched ROMs sheet
body:
## Objective
Create an Excel sheet listing all unmatched ROMs alongside their suggested correct dump filename, MD5, and patch URL.

## Design
- Add `_write_unmatched_sheet()` to `exporter.py`
- Make patch URLs clickable hyperlinks

## Implements
RA-DEV-054
